name: Denjang Jiege Keh (Spiced Crab Soup)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Soft bean curd; cut into 1/2 inch cubes
1 tsp Hot red chili powder; mix with 1 tablespoon water
recipeInstructions:
The people of Kwangju, where this recipe originates, enjoy food that is salty and chili-hot. The fermented Denjang paste forms the base of the seasonings, enhanced by chili and ginger. Fresh crabs absorb these flavors, making the soup highly appealing. I visited my teacher's home in August during a three-day pre-typhoon downpour. Sheets of rain fell as my taxi navigated to the outskirts of town. The rice fields shimmered green beneath the intense rainfall, creating a wild yet beautiful scene. Upon arrival, I was relieved to work comfortably on preparing the crabs and soup.
1. Combine water and Denjang paste in a pan. Simmer over low heat, covered, for 10 minutes.
2. Add bean curd and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
3. Incorporate ginger, onion, garlic, crushed chili with water, crabs, and zucchini. Continue cooking for another 15 minutes.
4. Serve the soup in four individual bowls alongside rice and side dishes.
